You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- World model: MOUSE doesn't affect gameplay objects, only consumes green bar. Blue rectangle moves with arrows but can't move down past rows 44-47. SPACE wastes energy. Need to reconsider the goal.
- Goal model: Maybe the goal involves the charcoal/magenta blocks at the top, or maybe I need to position the blue differently.
- Plan: Move blue UP back to the top area to investigate the charcoal/magenta blocks. Maybe they need to be moved or interacted with.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check where the blue is now and look more carefully at the top area near the charcoal/magenta blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check blue position
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    color = n['color']
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    if color in ['b', 'c', 'M']:
        print(f"{color}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Look at ascii around the charcoal/magenta area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n--- Top area rows 0-12, cols 30-44 ---")
for i in range(0, 13):
    if i < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][30:44]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the blue is at rows 16-19, cols 20-39. The charcoal is at rows 1-3, cols 36-39 and magenta at rows 4-7, cols 36-39. The blue's right edge (col 39) aligns with the right edge of the charcoal/magenta blocks.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the charcoal and magenta blocks form some kind of "goal" or "target" shape. The blue rectangle needs to be reshaped or moved to match this shape somehow.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. Maybe the blue rectangle is NOT the player. Maybe the charcoal/magenta blocks at the top are the player character, and the blue rectangle is an obstacle or platform. The arrow keys might be moving the blue rectangle out of the way, not moving the player.
But that doesn't make sense either because the charcoal/magenta blocks haven't moved at all.
Let me try a different approach. Let me look at what happens when the blue overlaps with the charcoal/magenta blocks. Let me move the blue UP and RIGHT to overlap with them.
Actually wait - the blue is at cols 20-39, and the charcoal/magenta are at cols 36-39. So the blue already partially overlaps horizontally with them. The blue is at rows 16-19, and the charcoal/magenta are at rows 1-7. There's a gap of rows 8-15.
Let me try moving the blue UP to get closer to the charcoal/magenta blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move blue UP toward charcoal/magenta blocks
# Blue at rows 16-19, charcoal at rows 1-3, magenta at rows 4-7
# Move up 2 times to get to rows 8-11, then 1 more to get to rows 4-7 (overlapping magenta)
action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
